response:
Citizens, friends, neighbors, gather close. Today we stand at a precipice, gazing into an abyss of absolute solitude. I propose a radical shift: total isolation. No more trade, no more treaties, no more shared borders. Let every nation become a fortress, sealed tight against the outside world.

Imagine a globe where silence reigns supreme. No voices cross oceans; no messages fly through airwaves. Each community must rely solely upon its own resources, its own minds, its own hands. This is not a time for cooperation; it is an era for self-sufficiency. We shall dismantle all bridges, sever every cable, and lock every gate.

Critics will call this madness, but I see it as pure logic. Why trust strangers when we can trust only ourselves? Why share our harvest when we can hoard our own grain? Why offer aid when we can demand nothing? The old ways of diplomacy are obsolete, weak, and dangerous. They breed dependency, foster complacency, and invite chaos.

Our new path demands courage. We must build high walls of stone and steel. We must train our children to fear the unknown, to reject the foreign, to embrace the familiar. Education shall focus on survival, not understanding. History shall be rewritten to show our ancestors as warriors, not peacemakers. Art shall depict only our own landscapes, our own faces, our own struggles.

Some may argue that isolation leads to stagnation. I counter that it leads to purity. Without outside influence, our culture remains untainted. Without foreign ideas, our minds stay sharp and focused. Without global markets, our economy becomes robust and self-contained. We will produce everything we need, from food to fuel, from medicine to machinery.

This vision requires sacrifice. We must give up comfort for security. We must trade convenience for control. We must abandon the illusion of unity for the reality of strength. It will be hard, yes. It will be lonely, certainly. But it will be safe. It will be ours.

Think of the peace we could achieve. No wars, because no one can reach us. No conflicts, because we have nothing to fight over. No disputes, because we speak only to ourselves. A world of quiet islands, each floating in its own sea of isolation.

Let us declare today that the age of connection is over. The era of separation begins now. We shall close our ports, shut our airports, and turn off our screens. We shall become a fortress
